original_hypothesis.title: AutomataDcapV3Attestation toggleLocalReportCheck Disables MRENCLAVE/MRSIGNER Verification
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The toggleLocalReportCheck() function flips _checkLocalEnclaveReport between true and false. When false (default), the MRENCLAVE and MRSIGNER trust checks in Step 2 of _verifyParsedQuote are entirely skipped. This means ANY enclave can produce a valid attestation, not just enclaves whose MRENCLAVE/MRSIGNER are in the trusted lists. A compromised owner could toggle this off to allow attestations from malicious enclaves, or the default-off state may be insufficiently restrictive.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: MRENCLAVE/MRSIGNER verification is a critical trust boundary ensuring only specific approved enclaves can produce valid attestations. The toggle mechanism allows this check to be completely disabled. Since the default is off (_checkLocalEnclaveReport = false), the enclave identity verification may never be active unless explicitly enabled.
deployment_assumptions: Owner must explicitly call toggleLocalReportCheck to enable
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: AutomataDcapV3Attestation constructor does not set _checkLocalEnclaveReport; defaults to false. SetDcapParams.s.sol calls setMrEnclave/setMrSigner but does NOT call toggleLocalReportCheck()

proof_of_signal.pattern: if (_checkLocalEnclaveReport) { ... check MRENCLAVE/MRSIGNER ... } — entire block skipped when false (default)

preconditions: _checkLocalEnclaveReport is false (default or toggled off by owner)
Attacker has access to ANY valid Intel SGX enclave (not necessarily a trusted one)
Attacker's enclave can produce valid DCAP v3 attestations

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: MRENCLAVE/MRSIGNER checks are conditional on a toggleable boolean
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: With check disabled, any enclave matching QE identity and TCB requirements passes attestation
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Untrusted enclave produces attestation to register as SGX prover, potentially submitting fraudulent proofs

economic_questions: What other verification steps prevent unauthorized enclaves from being accepted?
Can an attacker with any SGX hardware produce valid attestations when this check is off?
validation_requirements: Determine if TCB/QE identity checks alone are sufficient to restrict to authorized enclaves

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: Gas for attestation + SGX instance registration
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Depends on L2 proving rewards and fraud potential

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Any time when check is disabled

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All L2 users relying on SGX proof integrity
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Full asset exposure if fraudulent proofs accepted

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify if other checks compensate for disabled MRENCLAVE/MRSIGNER

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
